package handler

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
	"net/http"
	"time"
)

// Positive scenario for gin_context_passed_beyond_request_boundary
// Rule intent: Code that passes *gin.Context into goroutines, repositories,
// or long-lived service objects instead of extracting context.Context and request values.
func PositiveGinContextPassedBeyondRequestBoundary(c *gin.Context, db *gorm.DB) {
	var req struct {
		ID     string `json:"id" binding:"required"`
		Status string `json:"status"`
	}
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
		return
	}

	go func(ctx *gin.Context) {
		tenantID := ctx.GetHeader("X-Tenant-ID")
		var results []struct {
			ID       string `gorm:"column:id"`
			TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id"`
		}
		db.Where("tenant_id = ?", tenantID).Find(&results)
		ctx.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"processed": true, "count": len(results)})
	}(c)

	c.JSON(http.StatusAccepted, gin.H{"message": "processing started"})
}
